programmes.show_description: Founder of Talking Pictures TV and one half of the Footage Detectives, Noel Cronin, shares shows and films not to be missed throughout the end of March and the beginning of April.
Louie The Ring is Dead. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie meets his old flame Hazel and decides that it is time to make some quick money.
1957. Crime. Director: Joseph Losey. Stars Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern, Peter Cushing, Alec McCowan. David is an alcoholic who has 24 hours to save his son from being hanged for murder.
1955. Drama. Director: Anatole Litvak. Stars Vivien Leigh, Kenneth More & Eric Portman. A woman, unhappy in her marriage, has an affair with an RAF pilot, but will he be more than she can handle?
1965. The Gray Lady. Crime series produced by Aaron Spelling & starring Anne Francis. Honey goes after a master thief who stole an actress's jewellery while she was being interviewed on TV.
Trapline. 1956. Stars Scott Forbes, Lisa Gaye & Cyril Delevanti. Jim suspects that a fur company may be trying to drive him away by killing any Cajun trapper he does business with.
1956. Comedy directed by Maclean Rogers. Two dustmen (Bill Owen and Leslie Dwyer) come into possession of a valuable book but the donor's sister wants it back. Also starring Joy Nichols.
1934. Drama. Directed by John Cromwell. Stars Bette Davis, Leslie Howard & Frances Dee. A young man becomes attracted to a cold and unfeeling waitress who may ultimately destroy them both.
1947. Thriller. Directed by Alberto Cavalcanti. Stars Sally Gray, Trevor Howard & Griffith Jones. An ex-RAF airman joins a gang, but when a robbery goes wrong he is betrayed and framed for murder.
1952. Adventure. Directed by Ralph Murphy. Stars Louis Hayward, Patricia Medina & John Sutton. A retired buccaneer is forced back onto the high seas to escape execution and clear his good name.
The Hostages. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Annoyed by Tell's activities, Gessler issues orders to have the archer arrested but Tell has disappeared.
1970. Drama. Directed by Ken Hughes. Stars Richard Harris, Alec Guinness & Robert Morley. Politician and soldier, Oliver Cromwell, rises from the ranks to lead a civil war against King Charles I.
1954. Drama. Directed by John Eldridge. Stars John Gregson, Muriel Pavlow & Kieron Moore. Villagers take action when they find that a nearby RAF station is taking over a much loved bird sanctuary.
The Twisted Road. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Tom Farley. Cobb must deliver a prisoner safely to Brisbane in order for him to face charges after being accused of murder.
The King of Knaves Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Paul Stevens. A Middle Eastern leader is plotting to ruin the economy. Solo & Kuryakin adopt aliases to foil the plan.
The Rhyme and the Reason. Stars John Gregson, Alexander Davion and Daphne Anderson. A young man is accused of murdering his unfaithful girlfriend.
Murders in Aigues-Mortes - Crime à Aigues-Mortes. 2015. Director: Claude-Michel Rome. Stars Florence Pernel & Samuel Labarthe. Murder victims turn out to be suspects in an old unresolved crime.
Dive into the world of 'Hammer Glamour' with Caroline Munro tonight and explore the Lands Before Time in this sensational episode from The World of Hammer.
Lands Before Time. 1994. Narrated by Oliver Reed. A look at the unforgettable genre 'Hammer Glamour', which delivered a singular vision of history with liberal doses of both flesh and fantasy.
1960. Crime. Directed by Pat Jackson. Stars Gordon Jackson, Kenneth Griffith, Zena Walker and Dennis Waterman. After forgetting his ticket, a devious young boy causes trouble for the conductor.
The Jump-up Boys. Stars Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Budgie dons a white coat and takes a real job at last. But hearing that Budgie has gone straight, Charles suspects foul play.
1957. Crime. Directed by John Gilling. Stars Victor Mature, Anita Ekberg & Trevor Howard. An FBI agent will stop at nothing to catch the narcotics kingpin responsible for his sister's death.
1954. Drama. Directed by Lewis R. Foster. Stars David Niven, Martha Hyer & Hal Baylor. A young lady mistakes a hotel desk clerk for an oil millionaire she'd once met, and he decides to play along.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Living on a Volcano
programmes.show_description: 1967. A remarkable look at Ascension Island, an isolated volcanic island in the equatorial waters of the South Atlantic Ocean; home to many families, who sometimes work there for up to two years.
Silent Partner. 1961. Stars Stephen McNally, Robert Harland & Harold J. Stone. Investigative reporters Paul Marino & Jack Flood investigate various cases of industrial corruption. (S1, E11)
Another Neighbour. 1964. Stars Irene Ryan, Jean Willes & Susan Hart. The more prominent residents of Beverly Hills become more than a little affected when they sample Granny's spring tonic.
1949. Thriller. Directed by Oswald Mitchell. Stars John Stuart, Henry Oscar & Marie Burke. A psychic visits the home of his old friend and his wife but soon makes an unsettling discovery.
1953. War Time Drama. Lilli will not marry her love, an American reporter, until she becomes a star. Starring Lisa Daniely, Hugh McDermott and Sid James.
1938. War. Directed by Zoltan Korda. Stars Sabu, Raymond Massey & Roger Livesey. When the Khan of Tokot is murdered, his killer, the evil Prince Ghul, plots an uprising against British rule.
1954. Adventure. Directed by Tay Garnett. Stars Alan Ladd, Patricia Medina & Peter Cushing. A commoner disguises himself as the black knight to reveal those trying to overthrow King Arthur.
1957. Drama. Directed by David Eady. Stars James Hayter, Earl Cameron & David Hemmings. A murder suspect on the run must depend on strangers to help prove his innocence and clear his name.
The Raiders. 1956. Stars Alec Clunes, Jane Griffiths & Peter Hammond. New Providence is in danger from pirate raiders led by Charles Vane, leaving Governor Rogers to face a tough decision.
The Ordeal. 1976. Stars Jean Anderson, Patrick O'Connell & Richard Easton. The Riley family faces sobering news. Paul admits his feelings and gives April a final chance to save their marriage.
Special Edition. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Richard Bennet & Donald Bisset. A gang of career criminals believe they have the perfect alibi when they carry out a payroll robbery in Fleet Street.
1956. Drama. Directed by Jack Lee. Stars Virginia McKenna, Peter Finch & Kenji Takaki. While suffering the hardships of being a prisoner of war, Jean meets and befriends captive Australian Joe.
Founder of Talking Pictures TV and one half of the Footage Detectives, Noel Cronin, shares shows and films not to be missed throughout the end of March and the beginning of April.
1993. Documentary series that acts as a tribute to the greats of British cinema. This episode focuses on a wonderful tribute to Margaret Leighton.
Wheels/Grayling Fishing. 1981. Jack Hargreaves takes his strawberry cart to Mr. White the wheelwright for repairs. He then goes to the river Kennet for a spot of fishing for grayling.
1957. Night Crossing. Directed by Montgomery Tully. Starring Russell Napier, John Serret and Andre Maranne. The quiet of an idyllic port town is shattered when a body is found on the beach.
Let's Make a Killing. 1980. Stars Bryan Marshall, Shirley Anne Field & Clifford Rose. Suspicions grow about Burton's ''big deal''. Tony and Ray fly to Africa and uncover something significant.
1976. Drama. Director: George P Cosmatos. Stars Sophia Loren, Richard Harris, Burt Lancaster & Martin Sheen. Passengers of a European train can't escape after a deadly virus is planted.
1982. Drama. Director: Jan Worth. Stars Sandy Ratcliff, Paul Copley & Bernice Stegers. The stories of three women: a researcher, a prostitute and a part-time switchboard operator.
